metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the plan (and AC1) that `docker-compose.yml` must also be updated to replace `image: gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` with a `build:` stanza pointing at the new `Dockerfile.gerrit`, and that `compose-up.sh`'s plugin-seeding step (lines 84–90) must reference the correct image/build target.
findings.finding: The plan says 'Extend the Gerrit image (`FROM gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1`) — a Dockerfile under `infra/compose/`', but no Gerrit Dockerfile exists in `infra/compose/` (only `Dockerfile.reviewbot` is present), and `docker-compose.yml` currently uses `image: gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` (not `build:`). Creating a new `Dockerfile.gerrit` is net-new creation, and it also requires changing the `gerrit` service in `docker-compose.yml` from `image:` to `build:` — a change the plan does not mention.
findings.location: ## Scope: 'Extend the Gerrit image (`FROM gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1`) — a Dockerfile under `infra/compose/`'  / AC1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` to the edit-target list: replace (or supplement) the gerrit service's `image: gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` with a `build:` stanza pointing at the new Gerrit Dockerfile so the custom image is actually built and used.
findings.finding: The plan never names `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` as a required edit target, yet the gerrit service currently uses `image: gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` (not `build:`). Switching to a custom Dockerfile (the core of this story) requires changing that service stanza from `image:` to `build: { context: ..., dockerfile: ... }`. This is a direct, unenumerated consumer of the new Dockerfile.
findings.location: Scope section / AC-1; infra/compose/docker-compose.yml line 28
findings.suggested_fix:
In the gerrit service stanza, change `image: gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` to:
```yaml
build:
  context: ...
  dockerfile: infra/compose/Dockerfile.gerrit
```
or keep the `image:` tag for the resulting built image and add a `build:` block alongside it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docker-compose.yml` to the plan's explicit edit targets and specify that the `gerrit` service entry must be changed from `image: gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` to a `build:` stanza pointing at the new `infra/compose/Dockerfile.gerrit` (mirroring the `review-bot` service pattern already in that file).
findings.finding: The plan's Scope names 'a Dockerfile under infra/compose/' as the delivery vehicle for extending the Gerrit image, but does NOT name docker-compose.yml as a required edit target. The live docker-compose.yml uses `image: gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` with no `build:` stanza for the gerrit service; unless the compose file is changed to use `build:` (pointing at the new Dockerfile), the container continues to run the base image and the Python+g2p installation is never used — the integration silently does nothing.
findings.location: Scope section / infra/compose/docker-compose.yml line 28
findings.suggested_fix:
In docker-compose.yml, replace the gerrit service's `image:` line with:
```yaml
  gerrit:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile.gerrit
```
and add `Dockerfile.gerrit` (or equivalent name) to the plan's Scope file list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact mechanism for the container-start symlink step (e.g., 'add an `entrypoint.sh` that runs the symlinks then exec's the Gerrit process') so an agent doesn't have to choose between entrypoint override, compose `command`, or a wrapper script.
findings.finding: The mechanism for running hook symlinks at container start is unspecified — entrypoint script, docker-compose `command` override, or init container are all valid approaches but the plan defers this design choice to the executor.
findings.location: AC3 / Scope bullet: 'container-start hook-symlink step'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the scope bullet: 'Add an `entrypoint.sh` to the extended image that creates the $site/hooks/ symlinks pointing at the in-container g2p console-script paths, then exec's the upstream Gerrit entrypoint.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion that posts a `recheck` comment on the test patchset and confirms the `comment-added` hook triggers a second `workflow_dispatch` (the recheck→verify mapping), so the full user-facing recheck loop is verified end-to-end alongside the patchset-created path in AC5.
findings.finding: The `recheck → verify` flow (comment-added hook) and the change-merged hook are new user-facing behaviors but have zero acceptance criteria or test coverage — AC5 only validates the patchset-created happy path, leaving the recheck loop entirely unverified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5 (coexistence proof) / AC3 (hook symlinks) / AC4 (recheck → verify mapping)
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC5: 'After the initial workflow_dispatch fires, post a comment containing "recheck" on the same patchset and observe a second workflow_dispatch event dispatched by the comment-added hook, confirming the recheck→verify mapping is functional.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the concrete container-start mechanism for hook symlink creation (e.g., a Dockerfile `ENTRYPOINT` wrapper script or a compose `command` that runs `ln -sf` before exec'ing Gerrit) and confirm it runs on every container restart, not just first boot.
findings.finding: The plan specifies that hook symlinks must be created 'at container start' (because image-build-time symlinks are shadowed by the volume mount), but does not name the concrete mechanism — entrypoint override, compose `command`, or a new init script — leaving the idempotency guarantee unspecified for container restarts.
findings.location: AC: 'At container start, symlink the patchset-created / comment-added / change-merged console-script hooks into the volume-mounted $site/hooks/'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a thin entrypoint wrapper script to the Dockerfile (e.g., `ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/gerrit-entrypoint.sh"]`) that symlinks the three g2p console-script hooks into `${GERRIT_SITE}/hooks/` with `ln -sf` (idempotent) then `exec /entrypoint.sh "$@"` to the upstream Gerrit entrypoint.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether `/var/gerrit/hooks` needs a persistent bind-mount (like `/var/gerrit/.ssh`) or whether re-symlinking on every container start is the intended design, and correct the AC3 rationale to match the actual volume layout in `docker-compose.yml`.
findings.finding: The plan states 'image-build-time symlinks are shadowed by the volume mount' as the justification for creating hook symlinks at container start, but `docker-compose.yml` declares no volume or bind-mount for `/var/gerrit/hooks`. The seven volumes declared (`gerrit_git`, `gerrit_index`, `gerrit_cache`, `gerrit_db`, `gerrit_etc`, `gerrit_logs`, `gerrit_plugins`) do not include a hooks volume. Without a hooks volume/bind-mount, image-baked hook symlinks would NOT be shadowed — but they also would not persist across container recreations. The plan's stated mechanism (container-start symlink step) is correct, but the stated reason is inaccurate, and the plan must also address whether a `gerrit_hooks` volume/bind-mount is needed for persistence.
findings.location: AC3: 'symlink the `patchset-created` / `comment-added` / `change-merged` console-script hooks into the volume-mounted `$site/hooks/`, pointing at the in-container g2p paths (image-build-time symlinks are shadowed by the volume mount)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `docker-compose.yml` (and `compose-up.sh`'s volume-seeding loop) as edit targets needing a new `gerrit_hooks` bind-mount for `/var/gerrit/hooks`, OR explicitly specify that hook symlinks are created inside the custom image at a path not shadowed by any volume, reconciling AC-3's 'volume-mounted' language with the actual persistence strategy.
findings.finding: AC-3 states hooks must be symlinked into the volume-mounted `$site/hooks/` at container start, but `$site/hooks/` (`/var/gerrit/hooks`) is not currently a bind-mount or named volume in `docker-compose.yml`. The existing volumes list only covers git, index, cache, db, etc, logs, plugins, and .ssh — not hooks. Without a persistent or bind-mounted hooks directory, a symlink written at container start would vanish on container restart, or be inaccessible from the host for inspection.
findings.location: AC-3; infra/compose/docker-compose.yml volumes section (lines 42–55)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the Gerrit `hooks` plugin is bundled in `gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` (e.g., by running `docker run --rm --entrypoint sh gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1 -c 'ls /var/gerrit/plugins/'` and checking for `hooks.jar`) and document the finding in `infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md`; if it is not bundled, add an external download + sha256 entry to `install-plugins.sh`.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the Gerrit `hooks` plugin is 'bundled in `gerrit.war`' and enableable via `init --install-plugin` / copy with no external download. No file in the codebase (install-plugins.sh, plugins/README.md, gerrit.config, compose-up.sh, docker-compose.yml) mentions the `hooks` plugin at all — not as bundled, not as installed, not as enabled. This is an unverifiable assertion that is load-bearing for the entire g2p dispatch mechanism.
findings.location: ## What / ## Scope / AC2: 'Enable the `hooks` + `replication` core plugins (from `gerrit.war` via `init --install-plugin` / copy — no download or sha256 pin)'
findings.suggested_fix: docker run --rm --entrypoint sh gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1 -c 'ls /var/gerrit/plugins/' to confirm whether hooks.jar is present; if absent, add it to install-plugins.sh with a pinned GerritForge CI URL + sha256, matching the existing events-log/oauth pattern.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify (e.g., by inspecting `gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` with `docker run --rm gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1 ls /var/gerrit/plugins/`) that the `hooks` plugin is bundled in the war / already present, and record the confirmed enablement mechanism (`init --install-plugin hooks` vs. copy-from-war vs. already active) in `infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md`.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the Gerrit `hooks` plugin is a 'core plugin bundled in gerrit.war' enableable via `init --install-plugin` / copy with no download or sha256 pin — but no codebase artifact verifies this. `plugins/README.md` catalogues three plugins (webhooks, events-log, oauth); `hooks` is absent. `install-plugins.sh` installs only events-log and oauth. ADR-0010 confirms `replication` is WAR-bundled but says nothing about `hooks`. If `hooks` is not in the war, the executor must fetch an external jar — invalidating the no-download claim and leaving no sha256 pin.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC2 / Scope section ('Enable core plugins (`hooks` + `replication`) via `init --install-plugin` / copy — no download or sha256 pin')
findings.suggested_fix: Run `docker run --rm --entrypoint ls gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1 /var/gerrit/lib/` and `docker run --rm --entrypoint find gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1 /var/gerrit -name 'hooks*.jar'` to confirm bundling status, then add a row to `plugins/README.md` for `hooks` parallel to the existing `webhooks` entry, with confirmed provenance (war-bundled or external URL + sha256).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the hook symlink creation step uses `ln -sf` (or equivalent idempotent form) so it is safe on every container restart, and document the behavior when symlinks already exist.
findings.finding: The container-start symlink creation step has no idempotency guarantee: if symlinks already exist (e.g., on container restart), a plain `ln -s` will fail with 'file exists', leaving hooks potentially absent or partially created.
findings.location: AC #3 — 'At container start, symlink the patchset-created / comment-added / change-merged console-script hooks'
findings.suggested_fix: Use `ln -sf $(which patchset-created) $GERRIT_SITE/hooks/patchset-created` (and likewise for other hooks) in the entrypoint script to ensure idempotent re-creation on every start.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `infra/scripts/compose-up.sh` to the edit-target list and update the `GERRIT_IMAGE` variable (or remove the hardcoded reference) to use the custom-built image name after S3 switches the gerrit service to a Dockerfile build.
findings.finding: `infra/scripts/compose-up.sh` hardcodes `GERRIT_IMAGE="gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1"` (line 18) and uses it to seed plugins via `docker run --rm --entrypoint sh ... "${GERRIT_IMAGE}"` (line 88). Once the Gerrit image is a custom build, this variable will reference the wrong (upstream) image, causing the plugin-seed step to run against the unextended image. This consumer is not enumerated in the plan.
findings.location: Scope section; infra/scripts/compose-up.sh lines 18, 88

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define how hook stdout/stderr is captured and surfaced (e.g., Gerrit hook logging, container log driver) and add an observable signal (log line, metric, or alert) for g2p dispatch failures.
findings.finding: No observability instrumentation is specified for the new g2p failure point — hook stdout/stderr routing into Gerrit/container logs is not addressed, leaving operators blind to dispatch failures.
findings.location: What section / AC #5 (coexistence proof)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a minimal interim observability bridge in S3: specify the journald log pattern that g2p hook failures emit (or that a failed `workflow_dispatch` call produces) and either wire it into the existing `observability.sh` probe or add a placeholder CloudWatch alarm stub, so hook-dispatch failures are not completely invisible before S6 lands.
findings.finding: S3 introduces a new failure domain (g2p hook → workflow_dispatch fire) but defers ALL observability — metric, alarm, log pattern — to S6, creating an undetected silent-failure window between S3 go-live and S6 completion. During this window, dropped hooks or bad PATs leave Verified=0 with no alert.
findings.location: AC 'Coexistence proof' + epic S6 dependency description
findings.suggested_fix: In S3's implementation, add a g2p hook wrapper script that logs a structured `G2P_DISPATCH_ERROR` marker on non-zero exit; extend `infra/scripts/observability.sh` to grep for this marker and publish a `rebar/host:g2p_dispatch_errors` Count metric (same pattern as `voter_errors` in `monitoring_s4b.tf`). The S6 alarm then just needs to declare the CloudWatch alarm resource — the metric contract is already in place.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact config key and file for the 'recheck → verify mapping' (e.g., a `[trigger]` stanza in `gerrit_to_platform.ini` mapping the comment text `recheck` to a `verify` workflow dispatch input).
findings.finding: The 'recheck → verify mapping' is not explained: what configuration key, in what file, and with what value implements this mapping? This is a real design decision left unspecified.
findings.location: AC4: '+ the recheck → verify mapping'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the exact console-script / entry-point names installed by `pip install gerrit-to-platform` (check `pyproject.toml [project.scripts]` in the lfit/releng-gerrit_to_platform repo or run `pip show -f gerrit-to-platform` in a test container) and record the verified binary paths that AC3's symlinks must point to.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `pip install gerrit-to-platform` installs console-script entry points named `patchset-created`, `comment-added`, and `change-merged` that can be directly symlinked into `$site/hooks/`. This packaging claim is unverified in the codebase — no pyproject.toml, requirements file, or documentation in the repo confirms g2p exposes these as installable console-scripts vs. requiring a wrapper shim.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC3 ('symlink the patchset-created / comment-added / change-merged console-script hooks into the volume-mounted $site/hooks/, pointing at the in-container g2p paths')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify retry/backoff (e.g., g2p config or wrapper) and a hard timeout for the GitHub API `workflow_dispatch` call, and ensure hook non-zero exit is logged/alerted so operators can observe g2p failures.
findings.finding: The g2p `workflow_dispatch` HTTP call to the GitHub API has no retry, backoff, timeout, or graceful-degradation specified; if GitHub is unavailable, the hook process may hang or fail silently, and Gerrit operators will have no signal.
findings.location: AC / What section — g2p dispatch on patchset-created/comment-added
findings.suggested_fix: Add a timeout (e.g., `timeout 30s`) wrapper in the hook symlink invocation and document g2p's own retry config (if any). Configure Gerrit to surface hook stderr to its log. Add an alerting rule (e.g., log-based alert) on hook exit-code != 0.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete mechanism by which the GitHub PAT is kept out of the persisted `gerrit_to_platform.ini` — e.g., confirm g2p supports `%(ENV_VAR)s` / env-var expansion in its ini, OR write the ini to a non-persisted path (tmpfs or an in-container-only temp dir at startup), OR use a g2p env-var override that bypasses the ini for the token — and document this in AC4.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'no plaintext on disk' for the GitHub PAT in gerrit_to_platform.ini, but does not specify the concrete mechanism that enforces this on the boundary-crossing secret path. gerrit_to_platform.ini is a static INI file g2p reads at hook invocation time; if an entrypoint script writes the PAT from env into the ini at container start, the plaintext PAT lands on the gerrit_etc volume (bind-mounted to EBS-backed host storage at /var/gerrit/site/etc, readable by the host OS user). Neither g2p's support for env-var substitution in its ini nor an alternative delivery mechanism (e.g., ephemeral/tmpfs-only ini, wrapper script exporting env only, or ini written to a non-persisted path) is specified.
findings.location: AC4 — 'Write gerrit_to_platform.ini (GitHub PAT sourced via the container-env path from S4 ... no plaintext on disk)'
findings.suggested_fix: If g2p reads the token from an environment variable (check upstream g2p docs for `GITHUB_TOKEN` / env-override support): set the env var in docker-compose.yml from the `.env` and omit the token key from the ini file entirely. If g2p requires the ini, write it to a tmpfs or in-memory path at container start (e.g., entrypoint generates /tmp/gerrit_to_platform.ini from env, not /var/gerrit/etc/), so it is never on the bind-mounted EBS volume.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the top-level ticket title from '…on the Gerrit host' to '…in the Gerrit container' so it matches the H1, the body rationale, and all acceptance criteria.
findings.finding: The outermost title line reads 'Install gerrit-to-platform on the Gerrit host' while the plan body H1 and all substantive content say 'in the Gerrit container' and explicitly argue that host-side placement is broken. An implementer reading only the top-level ticket title may install g2p on the host, exactly the antipattern the plan warns against.
findings.location: Plan line 1 (outer title) vs. H1 and 'Why' section
findings.suggested_fix: Change the first line to: `S3 — Install gerrit-to-platform in the Gerrit container`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Decide and state the single plugin enablement mechanism (e.g., 'extract hooks.jar and replication.jar from gerrit.war during Dockerfile build using `java -jar gerrit.war init --install-plugin=hooks --install-plugin=replication`') rather than listing both options.
findings.finding: The plan offers two plugin enablement mechanisms ('init --install-plugin / copy') without choosing one, leaving a real implementation decision to the executor.
findings.location: AC2: 'from gerrit.war via init --install-plugin / copy'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command for the 'no plaintext on disk' constraint (e.g., `git grep -rE 'ghp_|github_pat_' . && echo FAIL || echo PASS` in CI, plus a note that the PAT is injected via environment variable at runtime, not baked into the image).
findings.finding: AC4's 'no plaintext on disk' constraint has no stated verification method, making it an unverifiable security claim.
findings.location: AC4: 'no plaintext on disk'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for each of ACs 1–4 and AC6 (e.g., `docker run --rm <image> python -c 'import gerrit_to_platform'` for AC1; `docker exec gerrit grep -r hooks $site/etc/gerrit.config` for AC2; `docker exec gerrit ls -la $site/hooks/patchset-created` for AC3).
findings.finding: ACs 1–4 and AC6 lack concrete proving commands; only AC5 specifies an observable end-to-end check. An executing agent has no stated method to verify image contents, plugin activation, symlink correctness, config validity, or ADR presence.
findings.location: AC1, AC2, AC3, AC4, AC6

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a 'Verification / integration test' sub-section to What/Scope describing how AC5's coexistence proof is obtained (e.g., a local test patchset push procedure, what logs/outputs confirm both votes fired, and under what environment this is run).
findings.finding: AC5 (coexistence proof) has no corresponding described deliverable or testing work in the Scope/What sections, making it an orphan criterion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5; Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Scope bullet such as: 'Verification: push a test patchset to the local Gerrit instance (with review-bot and g2p both active) and capture evidence of LLM-Review vote (review-bot webhook log) and workflow_dispatch event (GitHub Actions run URL) on the same Change-Id.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the Scope's dependency declaration to include the review-bot/LLM-Review system as a prerequisite for AC5, or explicitly note that AC5 is a post-integration milestone separate from S3 completion.
findings.finding: AC5's coexistence proof implicitly depends on the review-bot/LLM-Review system being operational, but the Scope declares only S2 (replication) and S4 (PAT) as dependencies, creating a cross-section mismatch between declared sequencing and the verification criterion.
findings.location: Scope — 'Depends on S2 (replication) + S4'; AC5

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback note (e.g., 'revert docker-compose to previous image tag / git revert the Dockerfile commit and redeploy') to the plan so the back-out path is stated, satisfying T4's reversibility requirement.
findings.finding: The plan replaces the running Gerrit container image (new Dockerfile extending gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1) with no stated rollback or back-out plan if the extended image breaks the Gerrit service.
findings.location: Scope — 'Extend the Gerrit image (FROM gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1)'; AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' sub-section: 'If the extended image fails to start Gerrit correctly, revert the Dockerfile commit and re-run `docker compose up -d gerrit` with the previous image digest. The $site volume data is unaffected by the image rollback.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a retirement/cleanup path for hook symlinks and config files (e.g., a documented removal script or entrypoint guard that removes stale symlinks if g2p is absent from the image).
findings.finding: No retirement/cleanup path is defined for the hooks config state (`gerrit_to_platform.ini`, hook symlinks, `replication.config` symlink) — if the feature is rolled back or replaced, these artifacts persist and continue firing.
findings.location: Scope section / AC #2, #3, #4

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove `change-merged` from AC3's hook list (or add an AC that specifies what CI behavior change-merged should trigger) — currently YAGNI: the plan's Why and all other ACs describe only patchset-created + comment-added gate flows.
findings.finding: The plan adds `change-merged` as a third hook alongside `patchset-created` and `comment-added`, but no AC or scope item describes what a `workflow_dispatch` on merge should do or how it relates to the CI gate. The plan's Why/What sections define the gate as patchset-created + recheck-comment flows only.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'symlink the `patchset-created` / `comment-added` / `change-merged` console-script hooks'
findings.suggested_fix: Drop `change-merged` from AC3. If post-merge CI is a future requirement, scope it explicitly in a follow-on story with a defined workflow step that consumes the dispatch.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the 'replication.config symlink' in Scope is a net-new file distinct from the S2 `replication.config` already placed by `setup-replication.sh`, or replace 'symlink' with 'extend setup-replication.sh' to stay consistent with the established cp+chown pattern.
findings.finding: The plan's Scope says 'replication.config symlink' but the existing codebase pattern in `setup-replication.sh` places `replication.config` via `cp + chown` (not a symlink). Introducing a symlink for the same file creates a second, divergent placement mechanism without justification.
findings.location: Scope section: 'replication.config symlink'; compared to `infra/gerrit/setup-replication.sh` lines 89–94

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify in the plan (and in AC4) the new SSM parameter name for the g2p GitHub PAT (e.g., `/rebar/prod/github-actions-pat`) and confirm that `fetch-secrets.sh` must be extended to fetch it and write it to `.env` as a named variable, coordinated with S4.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the GitHub PAT for g2p will be 'sourced via the container-env path from S4 — `fetch-secrets.sh` → compose `.env`; no plaintext on disk'. Reading `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` in full shows it fetches exactly six named SSM leaves (`anthropic-api-key`, `mcp-hmac-signing-key`, `gerrit-admin-password`, `gerrit-bot-token`, `github-oauth-client-id`, `github-oauth-client-secret`) and writes exactly those to `.env`. There is no g2p / GitHub Actions PAT entry. The plan treats this path as already established when it does not yet include the g2p credential.
findings.location: AC4: 'Write `gerrit_to_platform.ini` (GitHub PAT sourced via the container-env path from S4 — `fetch-secrets.sh` → compose `.env`; no plaintext on disk)'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
